rulings.Case Title: TAROUB H RUSNAK VS. JANICE SALAMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Subpoena To City Nation Bank
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 1 (Part 1 of 2). Plaintiff TAROUB RUSNAK'S Motion to Enforce Subpoena to City National Bank (Part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TAROUB H RUSNAK VS. JANICE SALAMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Taroub Rusnak'S Motion To Enforce Subpoena To City National Bank
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 1 (Part 2 of 2). Plaintiff TAROUB RUSNAK'S Motion to Enforce Subpoena to City National Bank (Part 2 of 2) The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. Plaintiff seeks enforcement of a post-judgment subpoena for business records issued to a third-party, City National Bank. While the subpoena purports to be issued pursuant to CCP section 1985, no trial or other hearing in this matter is scheduled. It is instead an effort at enforcement of a money judgment through discovery directed to a third party. Although third-party business records subpoenas are authorized by California's Civil Discovery Act, CCP section 2016.070 states that its provisions "appl[y] to discovery in aid of enforcement of a money judgment only to the extent provided in [CCP sections 708.010 through 709.030]." The statutory scheme for enforcing money judgments permits a judgment creditor to utilize only two procedures provided in California's Civil Discovery Act to obtain discovery. First, the creditor may (subject to certain limitations) propound to the judgment debtor written interrogatories in the manner provided by section 2030.010 et seq. of the Civil Discovery Act. (708.020.) Second, the creditor may (subject to certain limitations) demand that the judgment debtor produce and permit inspection of documents in the manner provided by section 2031.010 et seq. of the Civil Discovery Act. ( 708.030.) None of the other modes of discovery (such as third-party business records subpoenas governed by CCP section 2020.410 et seq.) are "provided in" sections 708.010 through 709.030; they are therefore unavailable. Further, this is not a case in which a third-party is subject to a court order for examination pursuant to CCP section 708.120 based on an affidavit showing possession or control of the judgment debtor's property. Accordingly, since the business records subpoena at issue here is not one of the procedures provided in CCP sections 708.010 through 709.030 to pursue collection of a judgment, plaintiff's attorney did not have authority to issue and serve the subpoena to City National Bank and his motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PORTSIDE MASTER HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION VS. PORTSIDE INVESTMENTS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, at 9:30 am, line 1. DEFENDANT SF OAKLAND BAY, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled. Regarding the statute of limitations, it cannot be determined from the face of the complaint that this claim is barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff has pled that it has been in possession of Parcel 1 since 1994. (Second Amended Complaint 13.) This is sufficient to state a claim for quiet title under Muktarian v. Barmby (1965) 63 Cal.2d 558, 560. Although the CCRs establish that other parties have an easement on Parcel 1 that does not definitively show that Plaintiff did not exclusively possess the property. Ankoanda v. Walker-Smith (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 610 is distinguishable because that case dealt with joint property ownership. Regarding misjoinder, the record owners of Parcels 2, 3, and 4 and each owner of a condominium unit in Parcel 4 are not necessary and indispensable parties because Plaintiff is not seeking to extinguish the easement. The demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff concedes that it does not intend to state a cause of action for breach of contract and this cause of action is duplicative of the first cause of action for quiet title. The demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Regarding the statute of limitations, the demurrer is overruled for the reasons discussed above under the first cause of action. Regarding failure to plead facts sufficient and uncertainty, the description of Parcel 1 as pled is sufficiently certain. Any dispute about the correct interpretation of the phrase "certain other areas" as contained in the CCRs is not properly determined on demurrer. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. It is permissible to separately state a claim for declaratory relief that depends on other properly pled causes of action. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is overruled because the demurrer to this cause of action was not separately stated in the demurrer. See CCP § 430.60. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (RAK/302)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST REUTER VS. BAKER PLACES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013 at 9:30 am, line 2. DEFENDANT BAKER PLACES, INC'S Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Of Summons. Granted. Plaintiff did not effectuate personal service on Defendant's Agent for Service of Process. A front desk receptionist is not "a secretary or assistant secretary or other person authorized to accept service." (CCP § 416.10(b).) Plaintiff has not met his burden to show the relationship between the Agent for Service of Process and the person served to establish substantial compliance. Defendant did not waive the right to seek dismissal by first filing its Answer to avoid entry of default. rulings.Case Title: IN RE: VORTEX MARINE CONSTRUCTION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013 at 9:30 am, line 5. RESPONDENT COWHEY PACIFIC DRILLING, INC'S Motion To Vacate Judgment. The motion is denied. The Court denies discretionary relief. Under Eternity Investments, Inc. v. Brown (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 739, respondent can rely on CCP 473 for relief from the petition to vacate deadline. Nevertheless, respondent failed to file this motion within a "reasonable time" as required by CCP 473. Paragraphs 21- 22 of the Losch declaration show that respondent knew of the arbitrator's relationship with plaintiff's counsel in October of 2012. Paragraph 24 discloses that respondent knew about the arbitrator's health condition in December of 2012. Respondent delayed filed the instant motion until April 2, 2013. In addition, the underlying grounds for relief are tenuous. There is little information regarding the relationship between the arbitrator and plaintiff's counsel and it appears that the relationship is too attenuated to warrant disclosure. See Nemeck & Cole v. Horn, (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 641, 646-647. Respondent also fails to show that disclosure of the medical condition was necessary. It is unclear whether the arbitrator even knew of his condition during the arbitration. The request for mandatory relief is denied because this case does not involve a default, default judgment or dismissal. See English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc., (2001) 94 Cal. App. 4th 130, 138. The request for equitable relief is denied. rulings.Case Title: YU-TAI FU VS. ACUPUNCTURE BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013 at 9:30 am, line 7. RESPONDENT ACUPUNCTURE BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Sustained without leave to amend. The petition for writ of mandate is time-barred. (Gov. Code § 11523.) Respondent issued its decision on November 15, 2012, and it became effective on December 15, 2012. (Petition, Exhibit A.) Petitioner filed the instant petition on March 11, 2013, which is untimely. CCP § 1094.6 does not apply to the instant action because the Decision was issued by a state agency, and not a local agency. Petitioner may not rely on his excuse or mistake for relief from the statute of limitations. (Kupka v. Board of Administration (1981) 122 Cal.App.3d 791, 794-95.) Respondent waived any defects in notice of the demurrer by substantively opposing the demurrer and filing an amended opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOSE G. MARTINEZ et al VS. DAVID LI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Reopening Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 4. Plaintiffs JOSE MARTINEZ, TATSIANA VETASHKINA, and EDUARDO ROJAS' Motion for Order Reopening Discovery Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown good cause to re-open discovery. The basis of plaintiffs request to re-open discovery is that they want to know why defendants sought trial de novo after an arbitration award. Plaintiffs contend that facts may come to light which would facilitate further settlement efforts and possibly identify "silver bullet" defenses that were not raised in arbitration. The reasons proffered do not constitute good cause in that they would apply in any case in which a party did not accept an arbitration award. Further, plaintiffs in their motion have not identified the specific additional discovery that they would undertake if discovery is reopened although their papers state that post-arbitration discovery requests were propounded. Accordingly, plaintiffs have not shown good cause to re-open discovery. Plaintiffs objections to the declaration of defense counsel are dismissed as the court did not rely on the declaration in reaching its decision. No sanctions are awarded as none were requested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VALLEY INTERNATIONAL ACADEMY VS. AMERICAN L & L EDUCATION, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT JEANNE LAW, WILLIAM LAW, DELTA KING ENTERPRISE, INC. and HENRY LAW'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Defendants' motion to strike portions of Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint improperly challenges the sufficiency of the facts alleged for all six causes of action. This is grounds for a general demurrer. See Ferraro v. Camarlinghi (2008) 161 CA4th 509, 528. The Court notes that if this were a properly filed demurrer, it would be appropriately overruled in light of paragraphs 5-7, 10-12, and 22 in the Third Amended Complaint. rulings.Case Title: RON LIPPIS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (IMPROPERLY SUED) Motion To Dismiss Action. Denied. The Court has discretion to accept a late filing of an amended complaint or to require a motion for leave to file it. See Harlan v. Department of Transportation (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 868. If the tardiness is inconsequential, the Court should deny the motion to strike or dismiss. Here, the Court declines to exercise its discretion to dismiss the action based on Plaintiff's failure to timely amend his complaint. A Second Amended Complaint was filed on May 8, 2013. rulings.Case Title: JOHN COWSILL et al VS. UMG RECORDING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Umg Recording, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, at 9:30 am, line 18. Defendant Umg Recording, Inc.'s Demurrer To Complaint. The Request for Judicial Notice of Ex. C is denied. Therefore, the demurrer to the first and second causes of action is overruled. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The Complaint does not state facts sufficient under the unlawful prong of the Unfair Competition Law based on failure to escheat. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (RAK/302)

rulings.Case Title: GREG CONTOS, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. KOKKARI LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories, First Set Of Inspection Demands And Req, For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF GREG CONTOS, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Special Interrogatories, First Set Of Inspection Demands And Request, For Sanctions. Granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 1. There is no reasonable expectation of privacy in contact information. Employees are potential percipient witnesses to alleged unlawful practices. Employees who have settled are likewise potential witnesses notwithstanding their release of ability to participate in settlement. See Puerto v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1242. Granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 2. There is no reasonable expectation of privacy in period of employment, which would relate to the ability of the witness to testify as to the applicability of the claimed unlawful practice to him or her. See reasoning for Special Interrogatory No. 1. Denied without prejudice as to Special Interrogatory No. 3. The rate of pay relates to potential remedies. The number of affected employees is not yet determined. Rates of pay should be discovered when and if a class is certified and the members of the class have been ascertained. Rates for these people only would then be discoverable. For now, the interrogatory is overbroad. Granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 9. See reasoning for Special Interrogatory No. 1. Granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 10. See reasoning for Special Interrogatory No. 1. Granted as to Inspection Demand No. 1. All non-privileged documents must be produced. Denied without prejudice as to Inspection Demand No. 2. See reasoning for Special Interrogatory No. 3. Granted as to Inspection Demand No. 3. This request is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Granted in part and denied in part as to Inspection Demand No. 4. "Depicting" is an overly ambiguous term. Defendant will produce documents that constitute, evaluate and/or discuss in substance its meal and rest period policies for hourly employees in effect during the alleged class period. Denied without prejudice as to Inspection Demand No. 5. The request for "all documents depicting" meal periods taken is overly broad. Denied without prejudice as to Inspection Demand No. 6. The request for "all documents depicting" meal periods not taken is overly broad. Denied without prejudice as to Inspection Demand No. 8. The request for "all documents depicting" the duration of meal periods is overly broad. Granted in part and denied in part as to Inspection Demand No. 13. Defendant will produce all manuals and handbooks applicable to hourly employees. Request for sanctions by Plaintiff is denied. In light of the tentative rulings, Plaintiff cannot be seen as having sufficiently prevailed and defendant's position is sufficiently justified. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW C. MCLAUGHLIN III et al VS. PALMER CITY-CORE STOCKTON STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 20, CROSS DEFENDANTS CYMAN PARTNERSHIP, ANDREW, III MCLAUGHLIN, THOMAS MCLAUGHLIN, ANDREW C MCLAUGHLIN III AND THOMAS O MCLAUGHLIN AS, ANDREW C MCLAUGHLIN III AND THOMAS O MCLAUGHLIN AS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Defendant has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief of the express easements. As pled, an actual controversy exists over the 1928 McEnerney Recordation and the 1943 Deed over light and air and the additional rights. A demurrer attacks the entirety of a cause of action, and not only part of a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: REAL ESTATE EBROKER, INC. VS. EDWARD LITKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 21, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT EDWARD LITKE'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is denied. The paragraph called "Time of Payment" is not on its face enforceable. There is a time for payment stated. The last sentence is subject to interpretation, leave a fact issue that cannot be determined on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
